headline: Could Apple's next 50 years of success lie in AI, foldable phones?
summary: Apple (AAPL) is celebrating its 50th anniversary on Wednesday, April 1. As tech consumers focus on the iPhone maker's latest wave of products, what could Apple's next 50 years look like?Ramon Llamas, the IDC Research director for mobile devices and AR/VR, joins Yahoo Finance Tech Editor Dan Howley and Josh Lipton on Asking for a Trend to discuss Apple's future in AI.

headline: Apple turns 50: A look at the iPhone maker's history of innovation
summary: Apple (AAPL) is celebrating its 50th anniversary on Wednesday, April 1. The tech giant has gone from designing computers to rocking the world with the iPod and iPhone and now dabbling in AI, catapulted by the leadership of founders Steve Jobs and Steve Wozniak and current CEO Tim Cook.Yahoo Finance Tech Editor Dan Howley comes on Asking for a Trend to talk about Apple's timeline of accomplishments and ability to maintain product quality.

headline: Analysis-SpaceX’s orbital data centers could face same hurdles as Microsoft’s abandoned undersea project
summary: SpaceX on Wednesday filed for an IPO that Elon Musk says will bankroll an effort to turn the rocket maker into an AI powerhouse, launching up to 1 million data‑center satellites into orbit to bypass power and water limits on Earth.  Microsoft had a similar ambition to escape land‑based computing constraints in 2015, when it lowered a shipping‑container‑sized data center onto the seabed off ‌Scotland, aiming to cut energy use through natural seawater cooling and tapping offshore wind and tidal power.  Microsoft’s "Project Natick," once touted as a potential breakthrough for the data‑center industry, successfully met all its technical targets but underwater data centers ‌were abandoned more than two years ago due to a lack of client demand and unviable economics, two sources with knowledge of the project told Reuters.

raw.headline: Analysis-SpaceX’s orbital data centers could face same hurdles as Microsoft’s abandoned undersea project
raw.summary: SpaceX on Wednesday filed for an IPO that Elon Musk says will bankroll an effort to turn the rocket maker into an AI powerhouse, launching up to 1 million data‑center satellites into orbit to bypass power and water limits on Earth.  Microsoft had a similar ambition to escape land‑based computing constraints in 2015, when it lowered a shipping‑container‑sized data center onto the seabed off ‌Scotland, aiming to cut energy use through natural seawater cooling and tapping offshore wind and tidal power.  Microsoft’s "Project Natick," once touted as a potential breakthrough for the data‑center industry, successfully met all its technical targets but underwater data centers ‌were abandoned more than two years ago due to a lack of client demand and unviable economics, two sources with knowledge of the project told Reuters.

headline: Amazon's cloud business in Bahrain damaged in Iran strike, FT reports
summary: Amazon's cloud computing operation in Bahrain was damaged after an Iranian ‌strike, the Financial Times reported on ‌Wednesday, citing a person familiar with the matter.  Bahrain's Interior Ministry ​said earlier in the day that civil defence teams were extinguishing a fire at a company facility following what authorities described as an ‌Iranian attack.  * The report of the strike came a day after Iran'sRevolutionary Guards threatened ​to target ​U.S. companies in theMiddle ​East, including Microsoft, ‌Google and Apple, inretaliation for attacks on Iran.

headline: Market uncertainty: Understanding the shift
summary: Geopolitical tensions, uncertainty over the Fed's interest rate decisions, and the growing costs of AI for big tech companies has Wall Street singing a different tune than many expected at the start of the year. On this episode of Trader Talk, Slatestone Wealth Partner and Chief Market Strategist, Yahoo Finance Head of New Myles Udland, Man Group Chief Market Strategist, Kristina Hooper, and Former Ellevest VP of People, Amanda Polcar, discuss where we go from here and what you should be doing to protect your money.

headline: Why Microsoft’s Growth Isn’t Saving Its Stock
summary: Microsoft (MSFT) is doing something unusual. It's beating earnings, growing revenue at 18% year-over-year, and yet its stock is down 36% from its peak. That doesn't happen to companies this size without a reason. The reason, in this case, is one number: capital expenditure (capex). Pre-AI era capex consistently ran at 25-27% of operating cash flow. The most recent full fiscal year saw that ratio climb to 47.4.

raw.headline: Why Microsoft’s Growth Isn’t Saving Its Stock
raw.summary: Microsoft (MSFT) is doing something unusual. It's beating earnings, growing revenue at 18% year-over-year, and yet its stock is down 36% from its peak. That doesn't happen to companies this size without a reason. The reason, in this case, is one number: capital expenditure (capex). Pre-AI era capex consistently ran at 25-27% of operating cash flow. The most recent full fiscal year saw that ratio climb to 47.4.
